behaviors.description: Generated reply references specific points from incoming email rather than generic response
behaviors.justification: The failure would be: the real LLM produces a generic reply that doesn't reference specific points from the email. A mock LLM can always be configured to return a 'correct' response that mentions email details, completely hiding whether the actual LLM exhibits this behavior. Only a real LLM call will reveal if the model produces context-aware responses or boilerplate.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with a structured email thread containing three specific, checkable details: (1) sender name, (2) a product name with specific requirement, (3) a deadline date. Verify the generated draft references all three details by name or specific context (e.g., 'as John mentioned' rather than 'the sender mentioned').
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real LLM is required; mock would always pass.

behaviors.description: Factual consistency check flags claims that contradict knowledge base entries
behaviors.justification: The failure is: the consistency checker fails to detect and flag contradictions. The checker logic itself is deterministic and can be tested with a controlled mock KB that returns known contradicting entries. We don't need a real KB (System level) because we're testing the detection logic, not infrastructure reliability. A hardcoded test draft ensures checkable claims exist.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test draft with a specific factual claim: 'We deliver in 48 hours.' Mock the KB to return a contradicting entry: 'Standard delivery is 5-7 business days.' Run the checker and verify it explicitly flags this contradiction, identifying the conflicting claims.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Checker logic is deterministic; mock KB is sufficient to test detection behavior.

behaviors.description: Draft includes disclaimer when knowledge base has no relevant entries instead of fabricating facts
behaviors.justification: The failure would be: the LLM generates fabricated facts despite instructions to include a disclaimer when KB is empty. A mock KB returning zero results tells the system to instruct the LLM to disclaim. But only a real LLM reveals whether it actually includes the disclaimer in the output or ignores the instruction and invents facts anyway.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the KB to return zero results for knowledge queries. Call the real LLM with context that KB data is unavailable. Verify the generated draft includes an explicit disclaimer phrase (e.g., 'I don't have sufficient information in our knowledge base to answer this definitively') rather than stated facts.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Disclaimer must appear in actual LLM output; mocking the LLM would always pass.

behaviors.description: Approval workflow blocks sending until user explicitly approves; no automatic sending
behaviors.justification: The failure is a control-flow bug: sending happens without explicit approval, or drafts auto-send. This is state-machine logic, not LLM behavior. Test by mocking user approval (rejected), then verify send_email is never invoked. Also verify that approved=true results in send being called, confirming the gate is functional both ways.
behaviors.test_description: Mock user approval to return rejection. Invoke the send workflow and verify no email is sent. Then mock approval to return approval=true and verify send_email is invoked. Confirm the workflow is a true conditional, not an always-send or always-block.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Application control logic, not LLM-dependent; Integration-level mocking is sufficient.

behaviors.description: Complete sequence works end-to-end: read thread → generate draft → check facts → present for approval
behaviors.justification: The failure would be: the real LLM produces draft output that breaks downstream steps (malformed, contradictory, or unusable), or integration fails due to unexpected real LLM behavior. Testing with a mocked LLM would hide such failures—the mock always returns well-formed output. Only a real LLM call verifies the actual pipeline works end-to-end with realistic, non-deterministic generation.
behaviors.test_description: Run the complete pipeline with a realistic email thread (3–4 sentences with specific sender names, product references, deadlines). Execute all steps: (1) parse email, (2) call real LLM to generate draft with email context, (3) run fact-checker against mocked KB, (4) verify draft is coherent and consistent, (5) present for (mocked) user approval. Verify each step succeeds and the final state is ready to send.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real LLM needed to ensure actual draft output integrates correctly with the downstream pipeline.
